name: Seven Pepper Chili
recipeIngredient: Used canned only if you must
2 Dried japanese chiles -- little skinny red ones
recipeInstructions:
This recipe is a lot of trouble and is recommended only for genuine chili fanatics.
1. Brown meat in lard until gray. Add onions and cook until clear.
2. Combine cumin, garlic, chilies pasillas, chiliie quebrados, Tabasco, salt, jalapenos, and tomatoes in a food processor or blender. Blend and set aside to steep.
3. Remove stems, membranes, and seeds from dried anchos, red chiles, and jalapenos peppers. Place these last three peppers in a small saucepan and barely cover with warm water. Bring to a boil over medium heat and simmer about 15 minutes.
4. Next, place softened peppers in the processor or blender. Save the water in which you boiled the peppers. You can use this water to add heat to the chili later.
5. Blend the peppers with the tomato mixture until smooth and add all this to the cooking meat and onions. Add sugar, beer, and chocolate to chili. Turn the fire down real low and simmer without covering - at least 2 hours.
6. Stir occasionally to keep it from sticking. Keep a quart of water nearby. Add as needed to maintain a soupy consistency.
7. Thirty minutes before serving, combine masa with enough water to make a thin paste and stir vigorously. Add this mixture to the chili while stirring frequently during the last half hour to prevent sticking. Taste and adjust salt and add more seasoned water if desired for heat.
